rulings.Case Title: GE CAPITAL INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS INC. VS. TELAVA NETWORKS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Charging Members Interest In Limited Liability Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF GE CAPITAL INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS INC.'S Notice Of Motion For Order Charging Members Interest In Limited Liability Company Denied without prejudice. Pursuant to CCP Section 708.320, the notice of motion must be served on the judgment debtor and either all of the members of the limited liability company, or the limited liability company itself. Plaintiff's proof of service shows that the notice of motion was only served on counsel for judgment debtor and the judgment debtor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GE CAPITAL INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS INC. VS. TELAVA NETWORKS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Assignming Right To Payments And Order Restraining Joseph Kristul
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF GE CAPITAL INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS INC. Notice Of Motion For Order Assignming Right To Payments And Order Restraining Joseph Kristul Granted, except as to item 4 in the notice of motion ("an assignment to judgment creditor of rights to moneys held in the custody and control of judgment debtor's personal and business accountants, personal managers, business managers, and any and all third parties of behalf of judgment debtors"). Moving party has not cited authority for the relief sought in item 4. No opposition filed. The Court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers with the exception of this language. The Court will add the additional language that "A right to payment may be assigned only to the extent necessary to satisfy the money judgment." CCP 708.510(d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH LUEBBERKE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Renewed Motion And Moton For Independent Psychological Examination
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (Amended) Notice Of Renewed Motion And Moton For Independent Psychological Examination Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to address following issues: 1. Motion for renewal appears appears to be proper pursuant to CCP §1008(b) and need not be heard by original judge (See Deaville Restaurant v. Superior Court (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 843); 2. Even if the original examination had gone forward, doesn't good cause exist pursuant to CCP §2032.320 for this examination as defendants do not have an expert who can testify? (See Kane & Rez, Cal. Pretrial Proc. & Forms (2012) §23:270); 3. Isn't an audiotape sufficient pursuant to CCP §2032.530? (See Bagleh v. Superior Court (2002) 100 Cal.App.4th 478); 4. Why shouldn't plaintiff and plaintiff's counsel be reimbursed for any costs or other losses sustained as a result of the aborted first examination? 5. What limitations, if any, should be imposed on this examination? Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ST. FRANCIS - ONE CRESCENT WAY CONDOMINIUM VS. TOP VISION DEVELOPMENT, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2013, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT BAY CITY MECHANICAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment/adjudication is denied without prejudice. Defendant fails to maintain its initial summary judgment/adjudication burden. Even if the Court takes judicial notice of the certificate of completion, BCM fails to demonstrate when the statute of repose commenced. Even if it true that the Certificate of Completion was issued on March 12, 2001 (Khadlikar Declaration, Ex. E), CCP § 337.15(g)(2) states that the trigger date runs from "the date of recordation of a valid notice of completion." In this case, the Court has no evidence regarding the date of recordation. BCM relies solely on the Certificate of Completion as the trigger date. See BCM's Memorandum of Points and Authorities at 6:4-6; 12:4-8 & UMF 18, 22-31. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. James E. Roberts-Obayashi Corp.'s Motion for Amendment to Cross-Complaint The motion for amendment is granted. The trial date is continued to March 17, 2014. The discovery cutoff is based on the new trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KURT HAGGSTROM VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To File Under Seal The "Under Seal Declaration Of George R. Kingsley In Support Of Pltff'S Mtn For Dismissal Of The Class Allegations Without Prejudice And The Individual Claim Of Kurt Haggstrom With Prejudice;"
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF KURT HAGGSTROM'S Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To File Under Seal The "Under Seal Declaration Of George R. Kingsley In Support Of Pltff'S Mtn For Dismissal Of The Class Allegations Without Prejudice And The Individual Claim Of Kurt Haggstrom With Prejudice;" Granted, for good cause shown. The Declaration of George Kingsley may be filed under seal. A redacted copy of the declaration will be filed in the public record. Only the settlement amount is to be redacted in the declaration and the attached exhibit. There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the rights of public access to this portion of the record; the interest supports sealing the record; the proposed order is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exists to achieve the overriding interest. Only the dollar amount of the settlement is sealed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN BRUCE VS. YOUNG K. CHO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Asnwers To Interrogatories And Demands For Production And Inspection Of Documents And To Produce Documents; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2013, Line 9, PLAINTIFF STEVEN BRUCE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Asnwers To Interrogatories And Demands For Production And Inspection Of Documents And To Produce Documents; And For Sanctions Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Within 20 days, Defendants shall provide further responses and produce responsive documents, without objections as follows: As to Defendant Cho, granted as to Form Interrogatory nos. 2.7, 12.4, 50.3, and 50.4, Special Interrogatory nos. 7 - 9, Demand for Production nos. 1 - 10 as to documents that have not already been produced on behalf of Majestic Scepter, Inc.; denied, as to Form Interrogatory no. 15.1, Special Interrogatory no. 12. As to Defendant Koepke, granted as to Form Interrogatory nos. 12.4, 50.4, and 50.5, Special Interrogatory no. 4, Demand for Production nos. 1 - 10 as to documents that have not already been produced on behalf of Majestic Scepter, Inc.; denied as to Special Interrogatory nos. 1, 2, 3, 5, and 8. As to Defendant Majestic Scepter, Inc., granted as to Form Interrogatory no. 12.4, 12.6, 12.7, 14.2, 50.3, and 50.4. As to the demands for production of documents, Plaintiff is entitled to a legible and viewable copy of the subject photographs identified as a contact size sheet (Majestic00048-Majestic00051); the parties are to meet and confer as to an efficient way to make those photographs available in print or digital form. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. The parties must meet and confer on discovery matters. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL COLLEGIATE STUDENT LOAN TRUST 2006-3, VS. ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC'S Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons Continued to September 17, 2013 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 30, 2013 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f).The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC VS. JOSEPH M. ALIOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Motion To Strike Complaint For Breach Of Contract And Breach Of Guarantees, And Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 30, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANTS JOSEPH ALIOTO, THE ALIOTO LAW FIRM, A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP'S Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Motion To Strike Complaint For Breach Of Contract And Breach Of Guarantees, And Enter Judgment Denied. Each document filed by LFG, through counsel, in this case was signed by a California attorney of record from Cooley LLP and clearly and conspicuously noted the pro hac vice status of Mr. Balber and Mr. Cross. These latter two counsel were subsequently admitted pro hac vice on August 12, 2013. There is no basis to grant the motion to disqualify counsel or any of the relief requested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
